perturbation.original_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account the stage of development or product lifecycle of other of [***] product candidates, safety and efficacy, product profile, cost of goods, the competitiveness of the marketplace, such company's patent position with respect to such product (including such company's ability to obtain or enforce, or have obtained or enforced, such patent rights), the Third Party patent landscape relevant to the product, the regulatory structure involved, the likelihood of regulatory approval, the likelihood and extent of anticipated or actual profitability of the applicable product, and other technical, legal, scientific and medical considerations. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who are held accountable for progress and monitor such progress on an on-going basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account the stage of development or product lifecycle of other of [***] product candidates, safety and efficacy, product profile, cost of goods, the competitiveness of the marketplace, such company's patent position with respect to such product (including such company's ability to obtain or enforce, or have obtained or enforced, such patent rights), the Third Party patent landscape relevant to the product, the regulatory structure involved, the likelihood of regulatory approval, the likelihood and extent of anticipated or actual profitability of the applicable product, and other technical, legal, scientific and medical considerations. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who may or may not be held accountable for progress, and not necessarily monitor such progress on an on-going basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" is changed to introduce an in-text contradiction. The change alters requirement (i) to state that employee(s) may or may not be held accountable for progress, and that progress is not necessarily monitored on an ongoing basis. This contradicts the original definition, which required accountability and ongoing monitoring. This leads to uncertainty in how the clause is supposed to be enforced on the parties, which is causing uncertainty to the level of efforts. The contradiction arises specifically in Section 3.1, where Aimmune is obligated to use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to achieve development milestones and obtain regulatory approval. This change will contradict Section 3.1 as it introduces ambiguity about the level of accountability and oversight Aimmune must exercise in its development activities, potentially undermining the milestone requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Overview of Development. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Aimmune shall be responsible for the Development of the Product as set forth herein. Aimmune, itself or with or through its Affiliates and Sublicensees, shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to perform the Development Activities for the Product to (i) achieve the development milestones set forth in Section 7.2, and (ii) obtain Regulatory Approval for the Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" to state that employees may or may not be held accountable and that progress is not necessarily monitored on an ongoing basis. This directly contradicts the original requirement for accountability and ongoing monitoring. Section 3.1 requires Aimmune to use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to achieve development milestones and obtain regulatory approval. The modified definition introduces ambiguity about the level of accountability and oversight Aimmune must exercise, directly undermining the milestone requirements in Section 3.1. Therefore a strong contradiction is present.

perturbation.original_text: 1.64 "Regulatory Approval" means, with respect to any biopharmaceutical product in any regulatory jurisdiction for a given indication, approval from the applicable Regulatory Authority permitting the manufacture, sale, distribution or Commercialization of such biopharmaceutical product in such regulatory jurisdiction for such indication in accordance with Applicable Law, including any Pricing Approvals.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.64 "Regulatory Approval" means, with respect to any biopharmaceutical product in any regulatory jurisdiction for a given indication, approval from the applicable Regulatory Authority permitting the manufacture, sale, distribution or Commercialization of such biopharmaceutical product in such regulatory jurisdiction for such indication in accordance with Applicable Law, explicitly excluding any Pricing Approvals.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Regulatory Approval" is modified to explicitly exclude Pricing Approvals. This change directly contradicts Section 4.1.2, which assigns Aimmune the responsibility for obtaining and maintaining Pricing Approvals. Because Pricing Approvals are essential for successful Commercialization in many jurisdictions, the contradiction creates uncertainty about Aimmune's obligations and the overall regulatory strategy. The in-text contradiction is causing the section to be contradictory as it does not explicitly state whether they will be responsible for them in this section, or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.2 Pricing Approvals. To the extent that a given country or regulatory jurisdiction requires Pricing Approval for sale of the Product, Aimmune shall (to the extent permitted by Applicable Laws) be solely responsible for (and shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts toward) obtaining and maintaining Pricing Approvals in all such countries and regulatory jurisdictions in which it obtains Regulatory Approval for Product, in its own name.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'Pricing Approvals' from the definition of 'Regulatory Approval,' while Section 4.1.2 states that 'Aimmune shall... be solely responsible for... obtaining and maintaining Pricing Approvals.' This creates a direct contradiction because the changed definition suggests that obtaining pricing approvals is outside the scope of 'Regulatory Approval' as defined, while Section 4.1.2 places the responsibility of obtaining these approvals on Aimmune. This is a structural flaw that introduces an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.4 Limitation of Liability. NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY FOR ANY LOST PROFITS, OR ANY SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, OR INDIRECT DAMAGES ARISING FROM OR RELATING TO ANY BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF ANY NOTICE OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. NOTWITHSTANDING THE FOREGOING, NOTHING IN THIS SECTION 11.4 IS INTENDED TO OR SHALL LIMIT OR RESTRICT THE INDEMNIFICATION RIGHTS OR OBLIGATIONS OF ANY PARTY UNDER SECTION 11.1 or 11.2, OR DAMAGES AVAILABLE FOR A PARTY'S BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS UNDER ARTICLE 12.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.4 Limitation of Liability. NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY FOR ANY LOST PROFITS, OR ANY SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, OR INDIRECT DAMAGES ARISING FROM OR RELATING TO ANY BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF ANY NOTICE OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. NOTWITHSTANDING THE FOREGOING, THE LIMITATION OF LIABILITY DOES APPLY TO THE INDEMNIFICATION RIGHTS OR OBLIGATIONS OF ANY PARTY UNDER SECTION 11.1 or 11.2, OR DAMAGES AVAILABLE FOR A PARTY'S BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS UNDER ARTICLE 12.
perturbation.explanation: The clause in Section 11.4 regarding the Limitation of Liability is modified to state that the limitation of liability applies to the indemnification rights and obligations of any party under Section 11.1 or 11.2, or damages available for a party's breach of confidentiality obligations under Article 12. This directly contradicts Sections 11.1 and 11.2, which outlines the indemnification obligations of Xencor and Aimmune respectively. This contradiction directly undermines the indemnification clauses, making it unclear whether a party can fully recover losses subject to indemnification, especially in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. The confusion that can stem from the contradictory clauses, can have parties dispute the level of liability as they may argue that they are not liable for something under said liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Indemnification by Xencor. Xencor hereby agrees to save, indemnify, defend and hold Aimmune, its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, agents and employees harmless from and against any and all losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses) (collectively, "Losses") arising in connection with any and all charges, complaints, actions, suits, proceedings, hearings, investigations, claims, demands, judgments, orders, decrees, stipulations or injunctions by a Third Party (each a "Claim") resulting or otherwise arising from (i) any breach by Xencor of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or obligations pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) the Development, Manufacturing, Commercialization (if applicable, after the Term) or the performance of a Clinical Trial for the Antibody or Product conducted by or on behalf of Xencor (or its Affiliates, licensees (other than Aimmune and its Affiliates and Sublicensees), sublicensees, or independent contractors), prior to the Effective Date or after the Term, provided that this Section (ii) is not intended to extend to strict liability Claims relating to the Product, (iii) [***], and (iv) the negligence or willful misconduct by Xencor or its Affiliates, licensees, sublicensees or subcontractors or their respective officers, directors, employees, agents or consultants in performing any obligations under this Agreement, in each case except to the extent that such Losses are subject to indemnification by Aimmune pursuant to Section 11.2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11.4 directly contradicts Section 11.1. The original text in 11.4 explicitly states that the limitation of liability does NOT apply to indemnification rights under Section 11.1. The changed text reverses this, stating that the limitation of liability DOES apply to those rights. This creates a direct conflict because Section 11.1 outlines Xencor's indemnification obligations to Aimmune, which are now potentially limited by Section 11.4, undermining the protection intended by the indemnification clause. This fulfills the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as it introduces a conflicting clause that creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
